Environment facts:
- First collect Debian release, enabled suites, pinning files, and current package candidates with read-only checks.

Evidence:
- Run `scripts/collect-env.sh` and `scripts/inspect-apt.sh linux-image-amd64`.
- Inspect `apt-cache policy`, current deb822 source files, and an `apt -s full-upgrade` simulation before changing backports.

Safe next checks:
- Do not make backports the global default. Verify the exact package that needs backports and install with `-t bookworm-backports` when appropriate.

Approval boundary:
- Requires user approval before changing APT sources or pins. Back up source and preferences files, verify package candidates, and rollback by restoring those backups.
